questions.text: The design of food premises provides adequate working space; permit maintenance & cleaning to prevent the entry of dirt, dust & pests.
The internal structure & fittings are made of non-toxic and impermeable material.
Walls, ceilings & doors are free from flaking paint or plaster, condensation & shedding particles
Floors are non-absorbent, non-slippery & sloped appropriately
Windows are kept closed & fitted with insect proof screen when opening to external environment
Doors are smooth and non-absorbent. Suitable precautions have been taken prevent entry of pests
Potable water (meeting standards of IS:10500 & tested semi-annually with records maintained thereof) is used as product ingredient or in contact with food or food contact surface.
Equipment and containers are made of non-toxic, impervious, non- corrosive material which is easy to clean & disinfect
Adequate facilities for heating, cooling, refrigeration and freezing food & facilitate monitoring of temperature
Premise has sufficient lighting.Lighting fixtures are protected to prevent contamination on breakage
Adequate ventilation is provided within the premises.
An adequate storage facility for food, packaging materials, chemicals, personnel items etc is available.
Personnel hygiene facilities are available including adequate number of hand washing facilities, toilets, change rooms for employees
Food material is tested either through internal laboratory or through an accredited lab. Check

Incoming material is procured as per internally laid down specification from approved vendors. Check for records (like certificate of analysis, Form E, specifications, name and address of the supplier, batch no., mfg., use by/expiry date, quantity procured etc.)
Raw materials are inspected at the time of receiving for food safety hazards.(Farm produce like vegetables, fruits, eggs etc. must be checked for spoilage and accepted only in good condition)

Incoming material, semi or final products are stored according to their temperature requirement in a hygienic environment to avoid deterioration and protect from contamination. FIFO & FEFO is practised. (Foods of animal origin are stored at a temperature less than or equal to 4 Degree Celsius)
All raw materials is cleaned thoroughly before food preparation.
Proper segregation of raw, cooked; vegetarian and non-vegetarian food is done.
All the equipment is adequately sanitized before and after food preparation

Frozen food is thawed hygienically. No thawed food is stored for later use. (Meat, Fish and poultry is thawed in refrigerator at 5 Degree Celsius or below or in microwave. Shellfish/seafood is thawed in cold potable running water at 15 Degree Celsius or below within 90 minutes.

Vegetarian items are cooked to a minimum of 60o C for 10 minutes or 65o C for 2 minutes core food temperature. Non vegetarian items are cooked for a minimum of 65 Degree Celsius for 10 minutes or 70 Degree Celsius for 2 minutes or 75 Degree Celsius for 15 seconds core food temperature.

Cooked food intended for refrigeration is cooled appropriately. (High risk food is cooled from 60 Degree Celsius to 21 Degree Celsius within 2 hours or less and further cooled to 5 Degree Celsius within two hours or less.)

Food portioning is done in hygienic conditions. High risk food is portioned in a refrigerated area or portioned and refrigerated within 30 minutes. Large amount of food is portioned below 15 Degree Celsius

Hotfood intended for consumption is held at 65 Degree Celsius and non-vegetarian food intended for consumption is held at 70 Degree Celsius. Cold foods are maintained at 5 Degree Celsius or below and frozen products are held at -18 Degree Celsius orbelow. (*Hot food is kept above 65 Degree Celsius and cold food is kept below 5O Degree Celsius but below 10 Degree Celsius upto 42 hours for not more than two hours only once.)
Reheating is done appropriately and no indirect of reheating such as adding hot water
or reheating under bainmaire or reheating under lamp are being used. (The core
temperature of food reaches 75 Degree Celsius and is reheated for at least 2 minutes at this temperature.)
Oil being used is suitable for cooking purposes is being used. Periodic verification of fat and oil by checking the color, the flavour and floated elements is being done.

Vehicle intended for food transportation are kept clean and maintained in good repair & are maintain required temperature.(Hot foods are held at 65 Degree Celsius, cold foods at 5 Degree Celsius and frozen item -18 Degree Celsius during transportation or transported within 2 hours of food preparation).
Food and non-food products transported at same time in the same vehicle is separated adequately to avoid any risk to food.
Cutlery, crockery used for serving and dinner accompaniments at dining service are clean and sanitized free form unhygienic matters.

Packaging material including wrapping material coming in contact with food is clean and of food grade. Packaging material used shall have Certificate of conformity issued by NABL accredited laboratory against the FSS (Packaging) Regulations, 2018. Check for records.
Cleaning of equipment, food premises is done as per cleaning schedule & cleaning
Preventive maintenance of equipment and machinery are carried out regularly as per the
Measuring & monitoring devices are calibrated periodically.
Pest control program is available & pest control activities are carried out by trained and
No signs of pest activity or infestation in premises (eggs, larvae, faeces etc.)
Drains are designed to meet expected flow loads and equipped with grease and cockroach
Food waste and other refuse are removed periodically from food handling areas to avoid
Annual medical examination & inoculation of food handlers against the enteric group of
No person suffering from a disease or illness or with open wounds or burns is involved in
* Food handlers maintain personal cleanliness (clean clothes, trimmed nails &water
Food handlers are equipped with suitable aprons, gloves, headgear, etc.; wherever necessary.
Internal / External audit of the system is done periodically. Check for records
Food Business has an effective consumer complaints redressal mechanism.
Food handlers have the necessary knowledge and skills & trained to handle food safely.
Appropriate documentation & records are available and retained for a period of one year, whichever is more

Is the production layout designed to prevent cross-contamination (raw to cooked flow)?
Are separate zones clearly marked for raw, cooked, and allergen handling?
Is adequate ventilation provided to prevent condensation and airborne contamination?
Are all light fittings in production areas fitted with shatterproof covers?
Are waste bins clearly labeled, lidded, and emptied at defined frequencies?
Are floor drains clean, fitted with traps, and free from blockages?
Is a master cleaning schedule in place covering all equipment and areas?
Are cleaning chemicals approved for food-contact use and stored in locked areas?
Are ATP swab tests or visual inspections performed to verify cleaning effectiveness?
Are cleaning verification records maintained and reviewed by supervisors?
Is a licensed pest control operator contracted with documented visit reports?
Are all bait stations mapped, numbered, and checked at each visit?
Is potable water tested for microbiological quality at scheduled intervals?
Are ice machines included in the cleaning schedule with documented records?
Do all visitors sign a health declaration and receive hygiene briefing before entry?
Are contractors provided with protective clothing and supervised in production areas?
Are all suppliers on an approved supplier list with valid FSSAI/ISO certifications?
Are incoming goods inspected for temperature, packaging integrity, and labeling accuracy?
Does every received item have a traceable batch/lot number recorded in the system?
Are internal lot codes assigned and linked to supplier batch numbers?
Are storage areas maintained within required temperature ranges with continuous monitoring?
Is FIFO/FEFO stock rotation consistently applied and verified?
Are all stored items labeled with product name, date of receipt, and use-by date?
Are decanted or transferred items re-labeled with complete traceability information?
Is there a designated quarantine area for non-conforming or suspect materials?
Are rejection records maintained with supplier notification documented?
Are production batch records maintained linking inputs to finished products?
Can each finished product batch be traced back to raw material lots within 2 hours?
Are WIP items identified with batch codes and time/temperature records?
Are rework procedures documented with full traceability of reworked material?
Are dispatch records complete with delivery details, batch numbers, and quantities?
Are transport vehicles inspected for cleanliness and temperature before loading?
Is there a documented product recall procedure with assigned roles and contact lists?
Can 100% of an affected batch be accounted for within the recall exercise time target?
Are customer complaints linked back to specific production batches for investigation?
Is there a trend analysis system for recurring traceability-related complaints?
Is a glass and brittle plastic register maintained and updated for all production areas?
Are all items on the register inspected at the defined frequency with records?
Are glass containers, gauges, and thermometers eliminated or protected in food areas?
Are overhead fixtures (lights, clocks, signs) fitted with protective covers?
Is there a documented glass/plastic breakage procedure displayed in all relevant areas?
Are breakage incident forms completed with details of contamination zone and product held?
Have all staff completed glass and brittle plastic awareness training?
Are refresher training sessions conducted at least annually with attendance records?
Is there a policy prohibiting glass and brittle personal items (phones, glasses cases) in production?
Are personal lockers provided outside production zones to store prohibited items?
Are metal detectors or X-ray systems in place for detecting foreign body contamination?
Are detection systems tested at the start of each shift with documented test results?
Are designated visual inspection points established at critical stages of production?
Are inspection lighting levels adequate (minimum 540 lux) at examination stations?
Are glass jars and rigid plastic containers inspected for damage before filling?
Are damaged or chipped containers segregated and disposed of with documented records?
Is the glass register verified by an independent auditor at least quarterly?
Are verification findings reviewed in management meetings with corrective actions tracked?
Are root cause analyses performed for all glass/plastic breakage incidents?
Is there a trend analysis dashboard tracking breakage frequency by area and shift?
